title: noronhia humblotiana
intro: Noronhia humblotiana, also known as the Humblot's noronhia or Madagascar ironwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. It is a large tree native to Madagascar, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. In traditional medicine, Noronhia humblotiana is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its bark and roots are used to make herbal teas and remedies.
